sections.paragraph:
Διόγνητος, ου, ὁ (found freq.; Demosth.; Polyb.; M. Ant. 1, 6; ins, pap) Diognetus, addressee of Epistle to Diognetus, Dg 1.

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 250
